title: Incorrect LEFT JOIN when FROM clause contains nested subqueries
description:
drh added on 2015-08-21 23:25:40:
The following query returns a single row rather than two rows as it should:
SELECT *
FROM (
    SELECT 'apple' fruit
    UNION ALL SELECT 'banana'
) a
JOIN (
    SELECT 'apple' fruit
    UNION ALL SELECT 'banana'
) b ON a.fruit=b.fruit
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 1 isyellow
) c ON b.fruit='banana';
The error seems to have been introduced by check-in [6df18e949d367629] on 2015-06-02 and first appeared in version 3.8.11.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE fruits (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, color TEXT);
CREATE TABLE colors (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, color_name TEXT UNIQUE);
SELECT * FROM (SELECT name FROM fruits) a LEFT JOIN (SELECT color_name FROM colors) b ON a.color = b.color_name;
key_bug_features: Incorrect LEFT JOIN with nested subqueries in FROM clause
